spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (document.querySelector) with runtime script (~2062 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Effectively no live controls — does not clear the interactivity bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (based, before, copy, dragenter, drop, dropeffect, during, effectallowed, move, releases, showing, style, user).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 50 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add live controls and a runtime hook
openQuestions.detail: Page must offer at least a try-it button that invokes the API and shows its return value or visible side effect; current state is closer to a static card.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("The drag and drop specifications require the "dataTransfer"'s object "dropEffect" attribute to have certain predetermined values on "dragEnter", "dragOver" and "dragLeave". Drag enter and drag over should have a "dropEffect" based on the cu") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/dnd.html#dndevents to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~2062 chars of script across 0 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 1 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
